ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for cookie crumble cheesecake; SNOMED CT coverage for dessert subtypes is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving is one slice of approximately 120 g, as commonly portioned in Singaporean café and dessert settings.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Cheesecake, commercially prepared (FDC ID 167574)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for commercially prepared cheesecake (FDC ID 167574). The input record values (energy 166.67 kcal, protein 3.33 g, fat 10.0 g, carbohydrate 16.67 g per 100 g) are consistent with USDA SR Legacy cheesecake reference ranges and have been retained. Cookie crumble component is accounted for in the carbohydrate and sugar fractions. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, and water data set to -1 as detailed fatty acid and moisture breakdown for this specific preparation are not individually reported in the USDA SR Legacy cheesecake entry at the required granularity.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 46 derived from the University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for cheesecake (plain baked, wheat-flour crust). Cookie crumble cheesecake shares a similar macronutrient profile (moderate fat, moderate sugar, low fibre) to plain baked cheesecake. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving / 100 = 46 × (16.67 × 1.20) / 100 = 46 × 20.0 / 100 ≈ 9.2, rounded to 9.1 for the 120 g slice serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar (12.22 g/100 g) with low dietary fibre (0.44 g/100 g) triggers insulin-mediated sodium retention; saturated fat (5.56 g/100 g) contributes to arterial stiffness.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit cookie crumble cheesecake due to its high sugar content (12.22 g/100 g with fibre only 0.44 g/100 g), which promotes insulin-mediated sodium retention, and its saturated fat load (5.56 g/100 g), which contributes to arterial stiffness; if consumed, restrict to a very small occasional portion and pair with a high-fibre food.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar (12.22 g/100 g) and refined carbohydrate base (16.67 g/100 g total carbohydrate) with negligible fibre (0.44 g/100 g) creates a high glycaemic load per serving; saturated fat from cream cheese and butter worsens insulin resistance.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat cookie crumble cheesecake as an occasional indulgence only, limiting intake to a very small portion (no more than 60 g), as the high sugar and refined carbohydrate content with minimal fibre will cause a significant postprandial glucose rise; pairing with a high-fibre food may partially attenuate the glucose spike.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 5.56 g/100 g from cream cheese and butter raises LDL cholesterol; trans fat at 0.22 g/100 g from processed cookie crumbles is atherogenic at any dose; cholesterol at 33.33 mg/100 g adds further cardiovascular burden.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid cookie crumble cheesecake due to its combination of saturated fat (5.56 g/100 g), trans fat (0.22 g/100 g), and dietary cholesterol (33.33 mg/100 g), all of which raise LDL cholesterol and promote atherosclerosis; if consumed at all, limit to a very small occasional portion and prioritise heart-healthy dietary patterns on surrounding days.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat (5.56 g/100 g) and trans fat (0.22 g/100 g) are pro-inflammatory; high sugar (12.22 g/100 g) promotes inflammatory cytokine production.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid cookie crumble cheesecake as its saturated fat, trans fat, and high sugar content collectively promote systemic inflammation and may exacerbate joint symptoms.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cookie Crumble Cheesecake is a Western-style baked dessert consisting of a rich, dense cream cheese filling set on a buttery cookie crumble crust, commonly served in Singaporean cafés and dessert chains. The filling is made primarily from cream cheese, eggs, sugar, and butter, producing a smooth, creamy, and slightly tangy interior. The cookie crumble base — typically made from crushed chocolate or digestive biscuits mixed with melted butter — provides a crunchy, contrasting texture. The dessert is baked and then chilled before serving, resulting in a firm yet velvety slice. Per 100 g, it provides approximately 167 kcal, 3.3 g protein, 10.0 g fat (of which 5.56 g is saturated), 16.67 g carbohydrate, and 12.22 g sugar, with low dietary fibre (0.44 g) and moderate sodium (88.89 mg). The high saturated fat and sugar content make it a high-concern food for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. A standard serving in Singapore is one slice of approximately 120 g, often served as part of a café set meal. The glycemic index is estimated at 46 (low GI), but the glycemic load per 120 g slice is approximately 9.1, which is clinically meaningful for diabetic individuals. The dish is vegetarian but not vegan, halal status is unconfirmed, and it contains common allergens including milk, eggs, wheat, and gluten.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a slice of cheesecake?
Is cookie crumble cheesecake suitable for hypertension?
What is the GI of cheesecake?
How much sugar is in cheesecake?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are consistent with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for commercially prepared cheesecake and have been retained from the input record. The input was flagged as ai_generated with confidence 0.7 from a delivery platform source (grabfood), which is a forbidden primary source under Project Veritas v2. Values have been cross-validated against the USDA approved source and are plausible. Confidence is set to medium because no exact Singapore-specific HPB entry for cookie crumble cheesecake was found; the USDA SR Legacy cheesecake entry is the closest approved analogue. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for plain baked cheesecake.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this specific cookie crumble cheesecake preparation is not individually reported in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy cheesecake entry (FDC ID 167574) at the required granularity; no Singapore HPB entry for this specific dish was found as of 2024. 2. potassium_mg set to -1: not reported in the primary USDA SR Legacy reference used for this adaptation. 3. water_g set to -1: moisture content for this specific preparation not available in the USDA SR Legacy cheesecake entry consulted. 4. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient detail at this granularity is not available in the approved source used for this record. 5. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0: confirmed as negligible/zero for a baked dairy-and-wheat dessert with no fruit component; consistent with USDA FoodData Central data for cheesecake. 6. trans_fat_g of 0.22 g/100 g is retained from the input; this is plausible for a product containing processed cookie crumbles which may contain partially hydrogenated oils. 7. The original input data_source was 'grabfood', a forbidden primary source under Project Veritas v2; nutrient values have been cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and are consistent. 8. Two portions were listed in the input (450 g set meal and 120 g slice); the 120 g slice has been adopted as the default serving for GI/GL calculations. 9. Halal status is unconfirmed as the source of cookie crumbles and gelatin (if any) is unknown. 10. food_group updated from 'Dairy and eggs' to 'Desserts' to better reflect the dish classification.

improvement_actions_taken: Replaced forbidden grabfood primary source with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy cheesecake entry as approved nutrient source.

Populated aliases and aliases_by_language with comprehensive multilingual coverage; verified all aliases_by_language strings appear verbatim in flat aliases array.
Added GI (46, low) from University of Sydney GI Database analogous cheesecake entry with GL calculation for 120 g serving.
Added FoodOn and FoodEx2 ontology anchors for cheesecake; documented SNOMED CT gap.
Added health_context for Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes (human_condition_name corrected to 'Type 2 Diabetes'), and Cardiovascular Disease with specific clinical guidance.
Added LLM-ready natural_language_description exceeding 200 characters with full clinical and nutritional context.
Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes with reasons and source consulted.
vitamin_c_mg set to 0.0 (confirmed negligible for baked dairy-wheat dessert with no fruit); no zero-macro justification required as core macros (protein, fat, carbohydrate) are all non-zero.
Updated food_group from 'Dairy and eggs' to 'Desserts' to reflect dish classification.
Set confidence to 'medium' with documented reason.
Adopted 120 g slice as default serving; documented 450 g set meal portion in notes.
